description_long: This is a small, single-story house with a steep, hipped roof covered in reddish-brown tiles and a central brick chimney. The exterior walls are finished in a weathered, light gray plaster, and the windows are symmetrically arranged with white frames and multiple panes. The front facade features a simple central door flanked by two windows, and a small, ornate guard booth stands to the right of the entrance. A row of rifles is displayed upright in front of the house, suggesting a historical or ceremonial context. The house has a modest, utilitarian appearance with no visible porch or decorative trim, and the roof overhangs slightly beyond the walls. The overall design is reminiscent of early colonial or rural European architecture.
description: A small, single-story house with a steep, tiled hipped roof, central chimney, and symmetrical windows. The weathered gray exterior and simple entryway are complemented by a guard booth and a row of rifles in front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, two windows, a guard booth to the right, and a row of rifles lined up in front.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting a single window, the guard booth, and the row of rifles extending along the left side.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying three large windows and the full expanse of the tiled roof with the chimney visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a single window and the row of rifles extending along the right side.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple central door with no porch, flanked by windows and accompanied by a small guard booth
